description: FDQ (Floating-Point Dynamic Quantization) Pipeline Resource Corpus for SWEObeyMe
purpose: Provide comprehensive resources for understanding LLM architectures, quantization methods, model formats, GPU/CPU/RAM hardware limits, and inference engines to enable safe FDQ transforms and performance envelope reasoning.

categories.name: llm_architecture
categories.description: LLM architecture and mathematical foundations including transformers, attention mechanisms, positional encodings, normalization, and activation functions

documents.title: Attention Is All You Need
documents.author: Ashish Vaswani, Noam Shazeer, Niki Parmar, Jakob Uszkoreit, Llion Jones, Aidan N. Gomez, Lukasz Kaiser, Illia Polosukhin
documents.description: Foundational transformer paper introducing self-attention mechanism, multi-head attention, encoder-decoder architecture, and position-wise feed-forward networks
documents.relevance: Core architecture foundation for all modern LLMs

documents.title: Llama 2: Open Foundation and Fine-Tuned Chat Models
documents.author: Hugo Touvron, Thibaut Lavril, Giza Izacard, Xavier Martinet, Marie-Anne Lachaux, Timothée Lacroix, Zhiqiang Tang, Armand Joulin
documents.description: Meta's LLaMA 2 architecture paper covering pretraining, fine-tuning, RLHF, safety alignment, and performance benchmarks
documents.relevance: Understanding modern open-source LLM architecture and training methodology

documents.title: Mistral 7B
documents.author: Albert Q. Jiang, Sabrina J. Mielke, Timothée Lacroix, Guillaume Lample
documents.description: Mistral 7B architecture featuring grouped-query attention (GQA), sliding window attention (SWA), and efficient transformer design
documents.relevance: Understanding efficient transformer architectures and attention optimizations

documents.title: RoFormer: Enhanced Transformer with Rotary Position Embedding
documents.author: Jianlin Su, Yu Lu, Shengfeng Pan, Bo Wen, Yunfeng Liu
documents.description: Introduction of Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE) as an alternative to sinusoidal position encoding, with mathematical properties and performance analysis
documents.relevance: Understanding RoPE used in modern LLMs for positional encoding

documents.title: Train Short, Test Long: Attention with Linear Biases Enables Input Length Extrapolation
documents.author: Ofir Press, Nitay Shirkey Bar, Omer Levy
documents.description: ALiBi (Attention with Linear Biases) method for enabling input length extrapolation without position embeddings
documents.relevance: Understanding alternative position encoding methods for longer context windows

documents.title: Root Mean Square Layer Normalization
documents.author: Biao Zhang, Rico Sennrich
documents.description: RMSNorm simplification of LayerNorm that removes mean-centering while maintaining performance, with 7%-64% speedup across models
documents.relevance: Understanding normalization layers used in modern LLMs like LLaMA

documents.title: Gaussian Error Linear Units (GELUs)
documents.author: Dan Hendrycks, Kevin Gimpel
documents.description: GELU activation function: x * Φ(x) where Φ is the standard Gaussian CDF, with performance improvements over ReLU and other activations
documents.relevance: Understanding activation functions used in modern LLMs

categories.name: quantization_formats
categories.description: Quantization methods and model format specifications including GGUF, GPTQ, AWQ, SmoothQuant, and other quantization schemes

documents.title: GGUF File Format Specification
documents.author: llama.cpp community
documents.description: Official GGUF (Georgi Gerganov Universal Format) binary file format specification for quantized LLM models, including type definitions, header layout, metadata section, tensor info section, and data layout
documents.relevance: Understanding the primary format used by llama.cpp for quantized models

documents.title: GPTQ: Accurate Post-Training Quantization for Generative Pre-trained Transformers
documents.author: Elias Frantar, Saleh Ashkboos, Torsten Hoefler, Dan Alistarh
documents.description: GPTQ one-shot quantization method using Hessian information to minimize quantization error, enabling 3-4 bit quantization of 175B parameter models
documents.relevance: Understanding GPTQ quantization method used for compressing LLMs

documents.title: AWQ: Activation-aware Weight Quantization for On-Device LLMs
documents.author: Linjie Li, Yuhao Li, Cheng Leng, et al.
documents.description: AWQ (Activation-aware Weight Quantization) method that preserves only 1% of salient weights in full precision to maintain accuracy while quantizing to 4-bit
documents.relevance: Understanding AWQ quantization method for on-device deployment

documents.title: SmoothQuant: Accurate and Efficient Post-Training Quantization for Large Language Models
documents.author: Guangxuan Xiao, Ji Lin, Mickael Seznec, Hao Wu, Julien Demouth, Song Han
documents.description: SmoothQuant training-free post-training quantization that smooths activation ranges to enable INT8 quantization of both weights and activations for large LLMs
documents.relevance: Understanding SmoothQuant method for INT8 LLM quantization

documents.title: RPTQ: Reorder-based Post-training Quantization for Large Language Models
documents.author: Zhihang Yuan, Xiaolong Niu, et al.
documents.description: RPTQ (Reorder-based Post-training Quantization) that addresses varying activation ranges across channels through reordering-based quantization
documents.relevance: Understanding RPTQ method for addressing activation quantization challenges

categories.name: gpu_architecture
categories.description: GPU architecture documentation for NVIDIA GPU families (Pascal, Volta, Turing, Ampere, Ada, Hopper) including precision specifications, tensor cores, and numeric behavior

documents.title: CUDA C++ Programming Guide
documents.author: NVIDIA
documents.description: Official CUDA programming guide covering GPU architecture, memory hierarchy, precision specifications, and programming model
documents.relevance: Understanding GPU programming model and precision specifications

documents.title: Pascal Tuning Guide
documents.author: NVIDIA
documents.description: Pascal architecture optimization guide with precision specifications, FP16/FP32 behavior, and performance tuning
documents.relevance: Understanding Pascal GPU architecture and numeric behavior

documents.title: Volta Architecture Whitepaper
documents.author: NVIDIA
documents.description: Volta architecture with Tensor Core introduction, FP16/INT8 precision specifications, and deep learning acceleration
documents.relevance: Understanding Volta GPU architecture and Tensor Core precision

documents.title: Turing Architecture Whitepaper
documents.author: NVIDIA
documents.description: Turing architecture with RT cores, improved Tensor Cores, and precision specifications for AI and graphics workloads
documents.relevance: Understanding Turing GPU architecture and precision improvements

documents.title: Ampere Architecture Whitepaper
documents.author: NVIDIA
documents.description: Ampere architecture with advanced Tensor Cores, sparsity support, BF16/FP8 precision, and improved AI performance
documents.relevance: Understanding Ampere GPU architecture and advanced precision support

documents.title: Ada Lovelace Architecture Whitepaper
documents.author: NVIDIA
documents.description: Ada architecture with improved tensor operations, DLSS 3, and precision specifications for consumer and professional GPUs
documents.relevance: Understanding Ada GPU architecture and tensor operation improvements

documents.title: Hopper Architecture Whitepaper
documents.author: NVIDIA
documents.description: Hopper H100 architecture with FP8 precision, transformer engine, HBM3 memory, and advanced AI acceleration
documents.relevance: Understanding Hopper GPU architecture and FP8/transformer engine

categories.name: cpu_architecture
categories.description: CPU architecture documentation for x86-64 and ARM processors including numeric behavior, SIMD extensions, and cache hierarchy

categories.name: memory_systems
categories.description: Memory system documentation including DDR3/DDR4/DDR5, GDDR5/GDDR6, HBM2/HBM3 specifications, bandwidth characteristics, and NUMA basics

documents.title: DDR5 SDRAM Specification (JESD79-5)
documents.author: JEDEC
documents.description: Official JEDEC DDR5 memory specification covering bandwidth, latency, power consumption, and technical requirements
documents.relevance: Understanding DDR5 memory characteristics for system memory planning

documents.title: HBM3E Product Brief
documents.author: Micron Technology
documents.description: Micron HBM3E high bandwidth memory product brief covering specifications, bandwidth, and applications for AI/ML workloads
documents.relevance: Understanding HBM3E memory characteristics for high-end GPU memory

categories.name: inference_engines
categories.description: Model runtime and inference engine documentation including llama.cpp, vLLM, TensorRT-LLM, and Hugging Face Transformers inference paths

documents.title: vLLM Documentation
documents.author: vLLM Team (UC Berkeley)
documents.description: Comprehensive vLLM documentation covering PagedAttention, continuous batching, model serving, and inference optimization
documents.relevance: Understanding vLLM inference engine for efficient LLM serving

categories.name: practical_guides
categories.description: Practical guides and blog posts on LLM quantization, GGUF usage, quantization tradeoffs, and real-world quantization applications

documents.title: A Visual Guide to Quantization
documents.author: Maarten Grootendorst
documents.description: Visual introduction to quantization concepts, methods, and tradeoffs with diagrams and explanations
documents.relevance: Understanding quantization fundamentals through visual explanations

documents.title: LLM Quantization Methods: GPTQ, AWQ, GGUF
documents.author: Cast AI
documents.description: Practical guide to LLM-specific quantization methods including GPTQ, AWQ, GGUF, SmoothQuant with implementation details
documents.relevance: Understanding practical LLM quantization methods and their tradeoffs

documents.title: A Guide to Quantization in LLMs
documents.author: Symbl.ai
documents.description: Comprehensive guide to quantization in LLMs covering methods, benefits, challenges, and implementation strategies
documents.relevance: Understanding LLM quantization landscape and practical considerations

documents.title: A Practical Guide to LLM Quantization (int8/int4)
documents.author: Hivenet
documents.description: Practical guide to LLM quantization covering GPTQ, LLM.int8, bitsandbytes, and implementation details for INT8/INT4 quantization
documents.relevance: Understanding practical implementation of INT8/INT4 LLM quantization

integrationStatus.llm_architecture: Complete - Core transformer and LLM architecture papers downloaded
integrationStatus.quantization_formats: Complete - Major quantization methods and GGUF spec downloaded
integrationStatus.gpu_architecture: Complete - NVIDIA GPU architecture whitepapers from Pascal to Hopper downloaded
integrationStatus.cpu_architecture: Partial - Access denied on official Intel/ARM documentation, need alternative sources
integrationStatus.memory_systems: Partial - DDR5 and HBM3E specs downloaded, need GDDR and additional memory docs
integrationStatus.inference_engines: Partial - vLLM documentation downloaded, need llama.cpp, TensorRT-LLM, HF Transformers docs

instructions.addingDocuments: Place resources in appropriate subdirectories. Update this index.json with document metadata including title, author, description, license, topics, and relevance.
instructions.downloadingSources: Download from arXiv, official vendor documentation, technical blogs, and open-source repositories. Prioritize papers with clear mathematical foundations and practical implementation details.
instructions.namingConvention: Use clear, descriptive filenames with underscores: paper_name.pdf, guide_name.html, specification_name.pdf
instructions.licenseCompliance: Include license information for all resources. Prioritize open-access papers, official vendor documentation, and properly licensed content.
instructions.fdqFocus: Resources should enable SWEObeyMe to: (1) understand LLM architecture and math, (2) understand quantization methods and formats, (3) understand GPU/CPU/RAM hardware limits, (4) design safe FDQ transforms, (5) compute performance envelopes, (6) reason about CPU vs GPU execution.
